events.title: 'US war on Iran not about the Iranian people: Europe can put the issue of human rights on the table'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Mahmood Amiry-Moghaddam, President of Iran Human Rights and Professor of Neuroscience at University of Oslo. He offers a stark assessment of the Iranian regime's response to recent unrest and wartime conditions. Professor Amiry-Moghaddam argues that the authorities have exploited international attention on regional conflict to intensify domestic repression. Civilians are effectively "risking their lives" so that "the world can know what is happening in Iran," underscoring both the scale of repression and the resilience of civil society under authoritarian rule.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'US war on Iran not about the Iranian people: Europe can put the issue of human rights on the table'

events.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
events.description: South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps country Mozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others. However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be officially linked to a wave of anti-migrant protests sweeping the country. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa

events.title: Iran says no 'tangible progress' in war talks, Trump says deal close
events.description: Iran's foreign minister said on Wednesday that negotiations to end the Middle East war had made "no tangible progress" after fresh US and Iranian strikes tested a fragile ceasefire. Kuwaiti officials said an Iranian drone strike on Kuwait International Airport killed one person and wounded 63, while President Donald Trump said talks could produce a breakthrough "over the weekend."
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Iran says no 'tangible progress' in war talks, Trump says deal close

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
events.description: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Follow live here from New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations

events.title: Japan: Modern-day samurais battle new foe: Climate change
events.description: It is a tradition that dates back a thousand years and has survived wars, earthquakes and even a nuclear disaster. But in recent years, Japan’s Soma Nomaoi samurai festival has found itself under threat from a new enemy: extreme heat.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Japan: Modern-day samurais battle new foe: Climate change

events.title: Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others
events.description: The IDF announced it killed a Hamas Nukhba terrorist who participated in the abduction of hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin during the Oct. 7 attack.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others

events.title: UK far right exploiting student stabbing attack 'to promote moral panic', expert says
events.description: Britain’s interior minister accused activists of hijacking a tragedy to stir up violence after police were attacked at a protest over the death of a teenager who was handcuffed as he lay dying while his killer stood nearby. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Aaron Edwards, Honorary Research Fellow at the University of Leicester, says that "we're seeing a populist challenge from the right which is feeding perceptions not founded in reality and promoting a moral panic".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK far right exploiting student stabbing attack 'to promote moral panic', expert says

events.title: Drone strikes apartment building in NATO member Romania as Russia attacks neighboring Ukraine
events.description: A drone entered Romanian airspace and struck an apartment building, causing an explosion and fire that injured multiple people, authorities said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone strikes apartment building in NATO member Romania as Russia attacks neighboring Ukraine

events.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
events.description: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission

events.title: Yemen: Hunger crisis deepens as funding cuts leave millions without support
events.description: Nearly half of the population in Government-controlled areas of Yemen are facing high levels of acute food insecurity with the crisis set to deepen further if international aid cuts continue, according to the latest analysis by the leading UN-backed global food security platform.

events.title: UN mission reports rising tensions along the Blue Line in Lebanon
events.description: Monday’s hastily convened meeting of the UN Security Council at the request of France in response to escalating violence in Lebanon between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants has underlined deepening international concern as the conflict intensifies, despite ongoing US mediation efforts.

events.title: Hamas struggles to fill leadership ranks as Israel hunts Oct 7 terrorists
events.description: Hours after an Israeli airstrike killed Hamas military wing chief Mohammed Odeh, Gaza's Eid markets were full, revealing a disconnect with Hamas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hamas struggles to fill leadership ranks as Israel hunts Oct 7 terrorists

events.title: Months after the regime crackdown, Iranians search for missing protesters
events.description: The deadly crackdown on the December 2025-January 2026 anti-regime protests saw many Iranians killed, tracked or detained. The US-Israel war on Iran has intensified the repression, but it has not stopped families from trying to trace their missing loved ones or from seeking justice for people who have disappeared into prisons and juvenile correction centres across the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Months after the regime crackdown, Iranians search for missing protesters

events.title: UN urges world not to forget Rohingya refugees as aid cuts threaten vital support
events.description: As the world approaches nine years since the mass displacement of Rohingya refugees from Myanmar into Bangladesh, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) has appealed to the international community not to abandon the 1.2 million refugees living in the country, most of them in camps in Cox’s Bazar.

events.title: Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink
events.description: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink

events.title: Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO
events.description: The UN health agency in Lebanon is verifying reports of strikes on a hospital in the southern city of Tyre on Monday, amid a concerning rise in attacks on healthcare in the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO

events.title: From Libyan deserts to 3D-printed guns: The weapons that never go away
events.description: Years after conflicts fade from the headlines, the weapons used to fight them often continue to circulate – crossing borders, fuelling crime and undermining an often-fragile peace. Now, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms and increasingly sophisticated trafficking networks are creating new challenges for governments worldwide.

events.title: Middle East war live: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement conditional ceasefire
events.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed Wednesday to renew their fragile ceasefire and create a number of “pilot” security zones inside Lebanon from which Hezbollah militants would be banned. In a joint statement released after a fourth round of US-mediated talks at the State Department, the two sides said the ceasefire “is contingent on a complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and the evacuation of all Hezbollah operatives” from areas south of the Litani River. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war live: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement conditional ceasefire

events.title: Nigerian court sentences four men to death over 2022 Catholic church attack
events.description: A federal court in Nigeria has sentenced four gunmen to death for a 2022 attack on a church that killed at least 50 people

events.title: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
events.description: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport

events.title: Kuwait labels Iran attack ‘heinous aggression’
events.description: Kuwait’s defence ministry has labelled an attack on the country’s international airport as ‘heinous Iranian aggression’.

events.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guards claim attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait
events.description: The already fragile ceasefire between Iran and the US has come under strain following a series of attacks from Iran in the Gulf and from the US on Qeshm Island. The Iranian attacks killed one person and injured 63 others. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid tells us more on the attacks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranian Revolutionary Guards claim attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait

events.title: ‘Women and girls cannot wait’: Lebanon’s crisis is putting lives at risk
events.description: Women and girls in Lebanon are paying an increasingly devastating price as violence and displacement continue despite a ceasefire, the UN reproductive health agency (UNFPA) warned on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Women and girls cannot wait’: Lebanon’s crisis is putting lives at risk

events.title: New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’
events.description: Minister at Singapore defence summit also reveals Australia to buy only secondhand Aukus submarines from US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The defence minister, Richard Marles, has said the “seabed is a battlefield” in a combative speech urging Beijing to be more transparent about its maritime operations, and taking aim at weak international controls over so-called “shadow-fleet” vessels. The warning came as the US, UK and Australia announced a new Aukus project to develop new underwater drone technology to protect undersea cables. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

events.title: Deadly attack on migrants highlights issue of labour abuse in Italy
events.description: Four migrants ​working as fruit pickers in slave-like conditions in southern Italy have been burned to death in a grisly murder case that is prompting fresh national soul-searching over labour exploitation. The migrants were found on Monday in ​a burned-out ‌van parked in a petrol station in Amendolara, in the southern region ⁠of Calabria. Surveillance cameras showed two people setting the car alight before running away. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.

events.title: 11 years after one teen's death sparked massive Argentine protests, a new case shakes the nation
events.description: The killing this month of a 14-year-old girl in central Argentina has shocked the country, triggering a groundswell of outrage and growing demands for government action against gender-based violence

events.title: Heroic ICE officers, staff member open up on growing dangers: ‘Am I going to die?'
events.description: ICE officers honored for heroism during the 2025 Dallas sniper attack speak out about growing threats and violence they say they now face nationwide.

events.title: Christian farming communities under siege as US report names Fulani militants Nigeria's deadliest threat
events.description: The USCIRF report states that Fulani militant violence in Nigeria surpasses that of Boko Haram and ISIS, with mostly Christians and also some Muslims being targeted in widespread attacks.

events.title: A US-Iran conflict was wargamed 14 years ago - here's how it went

events.description: Escalating violence in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) continues to impact civilians and efforts to fight Ebola, UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Tuesday.

events.title: World News in Brief: Updates from Gaza, the West Bank and Afghanistan, UN development reforms, change at the top of WFP
events.description: Families in Gaza living on or near the so-called Yellow Line controlled by the Israeli military have told the UN they live in constant fear of being killed or injured.

events.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
events.description: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

events.title: Does Israel have nukes? ‘Most of the world assesses they do,’ says Rubio
events.description: US secretary of state dodges question at congressional hearing on Washington's position on Israel's nuclear programme.

events.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills 22 people, officials say, as Moscow escalates fighting
events.description: Russian forces launched a massive aerial attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, and officials say at least 22 civilians were killed and 138 were wounded. The assault began overnight and continued during the day.

events.title: At least 21 killed in New Delhi hotel fire
events.description: At least 21 people have been killed after a fire tore through a hotel in the Indian capital New Delhi.

events.title: Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup
events.description: As World Cup fever rises in the Americas, countries are urged to strengthen measles surveillance and vaccination amid ongoing outbreaks across the region.

events.title: US ally Kuwait condemns 'brutal and ongoing Iranian attacks' after airport was hit

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola had 'big head-start,' WHO chief Tedros warns

events.title: Ukraine drones hit Saint Petersburg as economic forum starts
events.description: Ukraine has been stepping up long-range strikes as the war grinds on, with Russia's westerly second city now hit. The latest attack on Saint Petersburg takes place as an event long known as "Russia's Davos" kicks off.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine drones hit Saint Petersburg as economic forum starts

events.title: Kinshasa shuts down in protest against plan to reform constitution
events.description: In tonight's edition, many in Kinshasa stayed home in protest at the possibility of their leader running for a third term. Also, hundreds of Malawians and others in South Africa flee for their lives as groups of xenophobic locals reportedly go door to door in the Western Cape with their threats. And Senegalese fans are worried they won't make it to the football World Cup as visa applications have been systematically rejected since the end of last year.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Kinshasa shuts down in protest against plan to reform constitution

events.title: Syrians mourn a former chess champion as her death is confirmed 13 years later
events.description: Hundreds have gathered in Syria's capital to mourn former chess champion Rania al-Abbasi and her family, missing for 13 years

events.title: Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity

events.title: UN deplores another wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine
events.description: Overnight attacks in three key cities in Ukraine have left several civilians dead, scores more injured, and homes, hospitals and shops destroyed or damaged, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.

events.title: Cold case of missing news anchor rocked by claim of confession in roadside meltdown: report
events.description: A private investigator says he received a tip identifying a possible suspect in the 1995 cold case disappearance of Iowa news anchor Jodi Huisentruit.

events.title: What to know about U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats
events.description: The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What to know about U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats

events.title: UK Royal Navy helicopter crash leaves 3 dead
events.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the deaths were "utterly tragic," while the cause of the accident remains unknown.

events.title: Ukraine pushed Russia back in May for second month running, ISW finds
events.description: Ukraine recaptured more territory than it lost to Russian forces in May for the second straight month, according to data from the Institute for the Study of War (ISW). Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Christina Harward, Russia Deputy Team Lead at ISW, says that successful tactical counterattacks as well as ramping up of long range missile and drone strikes in Russian territory have been key in slowing down the advance in Eastern Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine pushed Russia back in May for second month running, ISW finds

events.title: Kuwait releases CCTV footage of deadly Iranian strike on airport
events.description: Kuwait has released surveillance footage of what it says shows an Iranian drone strike on Kuwait International Airport.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Kuwait releases CCTV footage of deadly Iranian strike on airport

events.title: Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim
events.description: North Carolina judge said Levi Mendez-Maldonado failed to show up in court – even after being told he had died in 2024 An immigration judge in Charlotte, North Carolina , recently ordered the deportation of a young man who was killed in 2024, citing his failure to appear in court. Judge Amy Lee ordered the removal of Levi Mendez-Maldonado in absentia on 21 May. Mendez-Maldonado, originally from Honduras, came to the United States as an unaccompanied minor at age 17 and was murdered in a shooting in November 2024. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim

events.title: World News in Brief: UN urges restraint as Gulf tensions rise, fear and uncertainty in Lebanon, hunger grows in the Sahel

events.title: UN General Assembly elects Bangladesh’s Rahman as next president
events.description: Bangladeshi Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman was elected President of the UN General Assembly’s 81st session on Tuesday after defeating Andreas Kakouris of Cyprus in a closely contested vote, positioning himself to steer the world body through a pivotal year marked by intensifying global crises, UN reform efforts and major leadership transitions.

events.title: FBI charges 35 in West Virginia drug and firearms operation, launches nationwide summer crime initiative

events.title: Celine Dion 'heartbroken' by death of Beauty and the Beast singer Peabo Bryson

events.title: Gunfire erupts in Mogadishu before protests against Somali president’s rule
events.description: Ex-Somali PM Khaire accuses government forces of attacking him before planned antigovernment protests in Mogadishu.

events.title: Cricket Canada suspended over allegations of gang-linked corruption
events.description: ICC’s decision comes amid growing concerns the team is being influenced by members of a notorious gang in India Cricket’s international governing body has suspended Canada over what it described as “serious breaches of its membership obligations”, dealing the latest blow to an organization that critics say has become a “laughing stock” within the sport. The suspension also comes amid growing concerns that one of Canada’s fastest-growing sports is being influenced by members of a notorious gang that operates with impunity from an Indian prison cell . Continue reading...

events.title: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
events.description: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’ Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance. Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi , Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off – with US encouragement – its pacifist outlook in place since the end of the second world war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China

events.title: Iran strikes Kuwait's main airport and kills 1 as ceasefire is tested again

events.title: Algeria court clears way for pardon of jailed French journalist Christophe Gleizes
events.description: French sports journalist Christophe Gleizes, detained in Algeria since 2024 on terror charges, may be eligible for a possible presidential pardon, his lawyers said Wednesday. Algeria's Court de Cassation, the country's highest appeals court, rejected prosecutors' calls for a tougher sentence, and noted the withdrawal of the defendant's own appeal.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Algeria court clears way for pardon of jailed French journalist Christophe Gleizes

events.title: Watch: Man attacked by bear at steel works in Japan

events.title: DRC Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, WHO chief says
events.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says the virus ‘had a big head start’ but that the response was catching up The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo could have begun as early as January, the head of the World Health Organization said, giving the virus “a big head start”. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus also said the response was being hindered by blanket travel restrictions and highlighted high levels of community mistrust and low levels of contact tracing as key concerns. Continue reading...

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
events.description: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled. The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
events.description: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama Ghana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned. The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity

events.title: 'We’re still behind' in Congo's Ebola outbreak even as testing improves, WHO says
events.description: The World Health Organization says Congo's Ebola outbreak had a head start, but testing is improving

events.title: ‘We are catching up’ – WHO chief on DR Congo’s Ebola fight
events.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Wednesday that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is showing signs of progress – but significant challenges remain in testing, surveillance, vaccine development and building community trust.

events.title: DNA from mystery juvenile male raises questions in Carnival cruise murder case against teen stepbrother
events.description: Federal investigators obtained DNA from a mystery juvenile in the Anna Kepner Carnival cruise death case as her stepbrother faces trial in September.

events.title: Atlanta train stabbing suspect faces new charge as feds weigh death penalty in great-grandmother’s killing
events.description: Georgia man faces federal charge carrying possible death penalty for allegedly stabbing a 66-year-old great-grandmother on an Atlanta MARTA train.

events.title: Ukraine attacks St Petersburg as Russia hosts its own ‘Davos’
events.description: Ukraine launched a series of air strikes targeting an oil facility and a naval air base near St Petersburg late Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine attacks St Petersburg as Russia hosts its own ‘Davos’

events.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
events.description: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” said Charles Mathenge, a taxi driver who lives near Laikipia Air Base, the proposed site in Nanyuki, 120 miles from the capital, Nairobi. “Kenya is our country, and we should be careful with it.” Continue reading...

events.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
events.description: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad

events.title: El Niño confirmed, set to fuel more extreme weather, says WMO
events.description: The UN urged all countries on Tuesday to bolster early warning systems after confirming the onset of El Niño, warning that the Pacific Ocean-warming phenomenon will bring above-average temperatures “nearly everywhere” and fuel more extreme weather.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 El Niño confirmed, set to fuel more extreme weather, says WMO

events.title: DHS confirms DUI charge against non-English-speaking driver in Virginia crash that killed 5
events.description: A naturalized U.S. citizen from China is charged with five counts of involuntary manslaughter after a Virginia crash that killed five people.

events.title: US military attacks Iran in 'self-defense strikes' over weekend
events.description: CENTCOM announced that it targeted Iran in "self-defense strikes" during the weekend, asserting that CENTCOM will defend American interests.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military attacks Iran in 'self-defense strikes' over weekend

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: One-ton cocaine bust reveals secret US-Mexico tunnel
events.description: Members of Jalisco New Generation cartel used fake retail store in San Diego as front for trafficking drugs, officials say Federal prosecutors have charged four suspects with trafficking more than one ton of cocaine for the Jalisco New Generation cartel using a fake retail store in San Diego as a front for a sophisticated tunnel that ran across the border to Tijuana, Mexico. The defendants include two Mexican nationals and two Americans charged with conspiring to traffic drugs across the US-Mexico border. The suspects, who range in age from 18 to 32, all face sentences that could put them in prison for life. One of them, Gregorio Epifanio Hernandez Lopez, also faces the charge of “constructing, financing or using unauthorized tunnels”. Continue reading...

events.title: Models suggest amplitude of El Niño in 2026 'as high or higher than any event in last century'
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Paul Roundy, Professor of Atmospheric and Environmental Sciences at the State University of New York at Albany, says that "the models and physical arguments based on tracking the progress of warm water suggest that the amplitude [of El Niño in 2026] could be as high or potentially higher than any event in the last century".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Models suggest amplitude of El Niño in 2026 'as high or higher than any event in last century'

events.title: Can Europe own its digital destiny? EU unveils tech sovereignty roadmap
events.description: The list runs long, strategic sectors where Europe's rarely the master of its own destiny, energy, manufacturing, even its nuclear deterrence. How about digital? Who even owns the personal data of its own citizens? The European Commission this Wednesday unveiling its tech sovereignty roadmap.

events.title: Deadly fire rips through New Delhi hotel
events.description: A fire has ripped through a hotel in New Delhi, killing at least 21 people including 18 foreign nationals.

events.title: Malaysia's social media ban for minors sparks privacy debate

events.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil despite US trade surplus
events.description: US claims world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in ‘unreasonable’ trade practices that ‘restrict US commerce’ Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil , charging that the world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in trade practices that are “unreasonable’’ and that “burden or restrict US commerce”. Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation”. The Brazil president also blamed the decision by the US administration on his rival in October’s elections, Flávio Bolsonaro , the senator who visited Washington last week. The senator is the son of former president Jair Bolsonaro, once nicknamed “the Trump of the Tropics” by his allies. Continue reading...

events.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
events.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

events.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX aiming to raise $75bn in record IPO
events.description: If completed, the unprecedented IPO would surpass the record $25.6 billion raised by Saudi oil producer Aramco in 2019 and could make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

events.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
events.description: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

events.title: Oddone hails EU-Mercosur accord's 'civilisational approach' beyond commercial and economic interests
events.description: Français Picard is pleased to welcome Gabriel Oddone, economist, professor and Uruguay's Economy and Finance Minister. He offers a striking defense of the social democratic model at a moment when much of Latin America appears to be espousing hard right socio-political and economic policies. Speaking at the OECD's Latin America Forum in Paris, Oddone argues that Uruguay's commitment to the welfare state, fiscal responsibility, and inequality reduction is not an ideological anomaly but the expression of a deep national consensus.

events.title: Raúl Castro turns 95 as Cuba celebrates enigmatic figure

events.title: Diphtheria outbreak: residents of remote NT community say health clinic has no hand sanitiser
events.description: There’s a three-week wait on test results in Yuendumu, near Alice Springs, and locals say NT Health has dropped the ball on telling locals what to do if they test positive Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A remote Aboriginal community at the centre of the Northern Territory’s diphtheria outbreak is struggling to cope with rising case numbers, with locals saying there is no hand sanitiser at the health clinic and limited information about how to avoid the disease or what to do if you test positive. There have been more than 240 cases of the once-eradicated disease reported in Australia since October, primarily in remote Indigenous communities in the NT, South Australia, Queensland and Western Australia , according to data released by the Australian Centre for Disease Control. One of the largest clusters in the NT is in Yuendumu, a community of about 700 people 300km from Alice Springs. Continue reading...

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?

events.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
events.description: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contacted police after discovering that his bank accounts had been frozen. events.title: Kidnappings, threats and ‘protection fees’: how can Mexico confront rise in deadly extortion?
events.description: With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of society It was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers frisked him and produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before. Luis, who asked not to use his real name for fear of reprisals, insisted that the drugs weren’t his, but the officers didn’t seem to care. They shoved him into the back of the police truck and drove into the night. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump threatens tariffs on 60 trading partners including UK and Canada over ‘forced labour’
events.description: Proposal for 10-12.5% levies, to also include EU, Taiwan and Australia, would allow US president to skirt court-imposed limits Donald Trump has threatened tariffs of between 10% and 12.5% on 60 trading partners including the UK, the EU and Australia over alleged forced labour failures, in the latest attempt to revive his signature trade policy. The EU immediately hit back, saying it expected the US to respect the tariff deal it entered into last July and arguing that stealth tariffs breached the spirit of that agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks

To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Australia politics live: Greens call for ‘bloody close look’ at new tax laws that give Jim Chalmers ‘Henry VIII powers’
events.description: Greens and Coalition oppose discretionary powers that would allow the treasurer to make rule changes to CGT and negative gearing after the legislation passes. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jim Chalmers ’ budget bill will face debate and a vote in the House today, and he poses the legislation as a “really simple choice” for the Coalition. As we know, the government has set up the bill as a wedge for the opposition, by including both tax changes to CGT and negative gearing and tax cuts for workers. Really simple choice in the parliament today: Labor lower taxes on workers, a fair go for first home buyers. The Coalition opposing both of those things. Yet another beat-up about the sensible, commonsense tax reforms at the core of the budget. Not unusual, as I said, for definitions to be settled this way, and the parliament can disallow them if they want. Continue reading...

events.title: Mapping the AI narrative in Kenya and South Africa's media

events.title: EU wants to favour European firms for most sensitive cloud, AI contracts
events.description: The EU on Wednesday proposed favouring European companies in public contracts for cloud computing and AI services in the most sensitive sectors as part of a bigger package to cut reliance on foreign firms. EU tech chief Henna Virkkunen insisted that "we are not closing anyone out" but told journalists that for "very critical" sectors like defence it was "very important" that Europeans provide the services. FRANCE 24's Yuka Royer reports.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: NSW motorists who use medicinal cannabis may soon be able to drive without fear of major penalty
events.description: Premier Chris Minns says changes would balance road safety and a more practical approach for medicinal cannabis users Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Motorists who use medicinal cannabis may soon be able to drive on New South Wales roads without fear of a severe penalty as the Minns government announces long-awaited reforms. events.title: German hotel apologizes for 'no Jews allowed' message
events.description: A Bavarian hotel has apologized after rejecting a booking by an Israeli customer by telling them that "there are no Jews allowed" at the hotel.

events.title: Taliban, Russia are cozying up to each other — why?
events.description: Russia and the Afghan Taliban have signed a deal on repairing Soviet-era and Russian-made weapons, a seemingly small move in a larger game involving the US, Pakistan and Moscow's efforts to curb Western influence.

events.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene

events.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
events.description: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

events.title: Germany news: Naturalizations reach record high in 2025
events.description: Naturalizations have increased for the fifth year in a row. Automotive industry firms turn a bit less pessimistic. And Germany is aiming for a seat on the UN Security Council. Follow DW for more news from Germany.

events.title: NASA ends MAVEN mission after Mars orbiter falls silent
events.description: NASA said on Wednesday it will end its mission studying Mars' atmosphere and evolution after losing contact with its MAVEN spacecraft for six months. MAVEN, which entered Martian orbit in 2014, was designed for a one- to two-year mission but operated for more than a decade before falling silent in December 2025.

events.title: SpaceX eyes $75 billion IPO, valuing company at $1.77 trillion
events.description: SpaceX, Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite company, said on Wednesday it plans to raise about $75 billion in an initial public offering, valuing the company at $1.765 trillion. The company will offer 555.6 million shares at $135 each, surpassing Saudi Aramco’s record $25.6 billion IPO in 2019.

events.title: Trump compares White House UFC arena to Eiffel Tower, hints it could stay permanently
events.description: In a video posted to his official Tiktok account on Wednesday, US President Donald Trump suggested he may never remove the enormous UFC arena that’s being constructed on the White House lawn for his birthday celebrations. He also compared it to the iconic Eiffel Tower in Paris, also initially meant to be temporary, but was kept on, though his take on the Eiffel Tower isn't quite accurate.

events.title: 'Both sides are committed' to US-EU trade deal, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer says
events.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer insisted that despite fresh US tariffs proposed this Wednesday, "both sides are committed to compliance" with the US-EU trade deal. The top US trade official said he saw "a lot of room" for continued compliance, notwithstanding Washington's new tariff proposals on imports of goods produced using forced labour.

events.title: Director Wim Wenders pulls 1975 film featuring then-teenager Nastassja Kinski in nude scene
events.description: German director Wim Wenders announced Wednesday that he would be pulling his 1975 film “Wrong Move” from circulation due to a scene in which actress Nastassja Kinski, then a teenager, appeared topless.

events.title: Child dies after inflatable castle tossed into air in Montreal park
events.description: A 3-year-old girl died from injuries she sustained when strong winds tossed an inflatable castle into the air during a party at a Montreal park
events.ticker_label: 💥 Child dies after inflatable castle tossed into air in Montreal park

events.title: Australian court bans man from contacting Norwegian princess studying in Sydney
events.description: A court in Australia has banned a 63-year-old man from contacting Norway's Princess Ingrid Alexander or her family for two years as she studies at university in Sydney

events.title: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra freed early from parole after receiving royal pardon
events.description: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra has completed his obligations related to a prison sentence

events.title: Colombian presidential candidate de la Espriella thanks Trump for endorsing his campaign
events.description: Colombian lawyer and presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, has thanked U.S. President Donald Trump for endorsing his campaign

events.title: Dutch court agrees to hear Greenpeace lawsuit against US energy company
events.description: Judges in Amsterdam have given Greenpeace a preliminary win in an anti-intimidation court case

events.title: Germany seizes tons of cocaine and suspects are arrested in Spain
events.description: German authorities have seized over 8 metric tons of cocaine from a container meant to carry cacao beans

events.title: Spanish hotel chain Meliá to shutter hotels in Cuba in latest blow to island's tourism sector
events.description: Spanish hotel chain Meliá has joined a growing list of companies with a long-standing presence in Cuba that are withdrawing or limiting their operations on the island after the U.S. announced new sanctions while upholding an oil embargo

events.title: Canadian government endorses plan to move whales from Marineland park to US, Spain
events.description: The Canadian government has endorsed a plan to move the last remaining captive whales from a shuttered theme park and zoo in Ontario to aquariums in the U.S. and Spain

events.title: Kyrgyzstan wins seat on UN Security Council for first time ever
events.description: A closely watched Security Council election delivered a mix of continuity and change on Wednesday, as Austria, Portugal, Trinidad and Tobago, Zimbabwe – and first-time member Kyrgyzstan, secured seats around the iconic horseshoe table.

events.title: Frontier passengers tackle man who allegedly choked off-duty flight attendant, tried to open door mid-flight
events.description: Juan Gabriel Reyes, 51, allegedly tried to open exit doors mid-flight and choked a flight attendant on a Frontier Airlines plane before being restrained.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Frontier passengers tackle man who allegedly choked off-duty flight attendant, tried to open door mid-flight

events.title: Brian Hooker may have left US, friend claims — but former prosecutor says feds likely have him in crosshairs
events.description: Sources speculate Brian Hooker may have left the U.S. via the California-Mexico border as mystery swirls around wife Lynette's disappearance.

events.title: Bakersfield, California hostage standoff ends after FBI kills armed sex offender
events.description: The hostage-taker is dead and the hostages are unharmed after an hours-long standoff at a bank in Bakersfield, California, according to police.

events.title: Karen Bass to face runoff election amid tough challenge from Spencer Pratt and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Meta leads largest-ever anti-scam operation with FBI and DOJ, resulting in 63 arrests
events.description: Meta led a sweeping anti-scam operation with the FBI, DOJ and Microsoft, resulting in 63 arrests and the removal of 1.4 million scam accounts.

events.title: Chicago cop killing suspect sent back to jail by judge who previously freed him under controversial bail law
events.description: The accused killer of Chicago police officer John Bartholomew is ordered back into custody in a carjacking case after the SAFE-T Act drew scrutiny.

events.title: Illegal immigrant flashes courtroom grin after allegedly killing baby, mother and grandmother
events.description: Illegal immigrant accused of killing a 2-week-old baby, mother and grandmother in California allegedly smirked during his courtroom appearance Monday.

events.title: Trump expands Cuba sanctions beyond US companies in major crackdown on foreign enablers
events.description: Trump administration rolls out unprecedented secondary sanctions on Cuba targeting foreign firms doing business with military-linked conglomerate GAESA.

events.title: Bodycam footage fuels backlash after police handcuff dying stabbing victim
events.description: Bodycam footage shows English police handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak after he said he was stabbed and could not breathe moments before dying.

events.title: Israeli official says EU sanctions reveal antisemitism hiding behind 'socially acceptable mask'
events.description: Israel's Deputy Foreign Minister accuses the EU of disguising antisemitism as anti-Zionism after sanctions hit Israeli civil society organizations.

events.title: Iranians speak out over possible Trump-regime deal
events.description: Iranians inside Iran express fear and frustration that a potential U.S.-Iran deal could leave the Islamic Republic intact despite economic devastation.

events.title: US ally answers Trump's call on Strait of Hormuz: 'Part of a diplomatic effort'
events.description: Romania allowed access to military bases for air refueling and defensive activities amid U.S.-Iran tensions, its foreign minister tells Fox News Digital.

events.title: Hidden tunnel discovered in Tijuana may have supported cross-border trafficking operations
events.description: Authorities uncover a 265-meter underground tunnel near the U.S.-Mexico border equipped with lighting, ventilation and an electronic transport system.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst

events.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
events.description: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.
